title: Hard-coded paths should not climb above the working directory
messageFormat: Avoid a hard-coded path that escapes the current directory with '..'; resolve the location from configuration instead.
summary: A literal file path that walks up the tree with .. segments assumes a fixed layout outside the location the program controls. It breaks the moment the code runs from a different working directory, is deployed, or is packaged, and it can let input steer access to unintended locations. Resolve the base location from configuration or a well-defined root and build paths beneath it rather than reaching outside with parent-directory traversal.
